title.rendered: 69% of Travellers Still Use Cash Abroad – Here’s Why It Matters

content.rendered:
For years, we’ve been hearing the same thing: cash is on the way out.

Contactless payments, mobile wallets and digital banking have transformed how we spend money day to day. But when it comes to travel, the reality is very different.

A recent industry report has revealed that 69% of travellers still use cash when they’re abroad, highlighting just how important physical currency remains when you leave home.

For anyone who travels regularly, this won’t come as a surprise.

Because while cards are convenient, cash still plays a key role in making travel smoother, easier and often less stressful.

Do People Still Use Cash Abroad?

In short, yes — and in many cases, more than you might expect.

The report shows that travellers aren’t choosing between cash or card. Instead, most people use both.

This “mix and match” approach reflects how people actually spend when they’re away:

  • Cards for hotels, restaurants and larger purchases

  • Cash for everyday spending, tips and smaller transactions

It’s practical, flexible and gives you a backup if anything goes wrong.

Why Cash Still Matters When You Travel

Even in destinations where card payments are widely accepted, cash continues to play an important role.

1. Not Everywhere Accepts Cards

Local markets, taxis and smaller cafés often prefer cash, particularly outside major tourist areas.

2. It Helps You Stay on Budget

Using cash can make it easier to keep track of what you’re spending day to day.

3. It’s a Reliable Backup

Cards can be declined, blocked or lost. Having local currency gives you peace of mind.

4. You Can Avoid Unexpected Fees

Using your card abroad can come with charges or less favourable exchange rates, depending on your provider.

Travellers Are Planning Their Travel Money Earlier

Another key trend from the report is that travellers are becoming more organised.

More people are now sorting their travel money before they leave, rather than leaving it until the airport.

And it’s easy to see why.

Planning ahead means you can:

  • Avoid last-minute stress

  • Have cash ready as soon as you arrive

  • Take advantage of more competitive exchange rates

Leaving it until the airport is often the most expensive option, and it’s something more travellers are now choosing to avoid.

Cash vs Card Abroad: It’s About Balance

The idea that travel is becoming completely cashless doesn’t reflect real-life experience.

Most travellers take a balanced approach:

  • Use cards for convenience

  • Carry cash for flexibility

Having both means you’re prepared for any situation, whether you’re paying for dinner in a city or buying something from a local market.

What This Means for Your Next Trip

The takeaway is simple: cash is still a key part of travelling abroad.

While digital payments are growing, physical currency continues to play an important role in how people spend overseas.

If you’re planning a trip, it’s worth thinking ahead and making sure you have some local currency with you before you go.

At Travel FX, you can order your travel money online for home delivery, making it easy to get organised ahead of your trip.

Because when you arrive at your destination, having cash ready to go can make all the difference.

itemListElement.name: 69% of Travellers Still Use Cash Abroad – Here’s Why It Matters

@graph.name: Travel FX Blog
@graph.description: Travel money news and tips from Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
